title: Kerala UDF cabinet: Congress finalises 9 slots, Shanimol likely to be Dy Speaker, allies differ on term
content: The Congress leadership is learnt to have made its decision for 10 ministerial posts in the UDF cabinet. KPCC president Sunny Joseph, Chief Minister-designate V D Satheesan and AICC General Secretary ( in-charge of Kerala & Lakshadweep) Deepa Dasmunsi are holding final round of talks to decide on remaining one ministerial berth for the Congress. Sources said that Ramesh Chennithala, Sunny Joseph, K Muraleedharan, A P Anilkumar, P C Vishnunadh, Bindu Krishna, M Liju, Chandy Oommen, Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan, T Siddique have been included in the tentative list. For the remaining slot, the Congress leadership may pick from Anwar Sadath, I C Balakrishnan or any MLA from Kozhikode. Shanimol Usman will be made the Deputy Speaker. There has been a demand to include one Congress MLA from Kozhikode in the cabinet after the party registered an impressive win in the district in the assembly polls 2026. The Congress won five seats in Kozhikode this time. The party is also dealing with questions on adequate community and caste representation in the cabinet. Meanwhile the Kerala Congress (Jacob) has communicated to the Congress that it was not willing to share the ministerial post on a term basis. "We are awaiting a reply from the Congress. We have asked for a full term, we hope the Congress will consider that," Kerala Congress (Jacob) leader and MLA-elect from Piravom, Anoop Jacob told Onmanorama. Meanwhile the Kerala Congress (Joseph) has accepted one ministerial berth and Chief whip post. The earlier demand was for two posts. In the past two days, choices were made considering religious, regional and communal equations. The final list will be cleared by the High Command and it will be submitted to the Kerala Governor. The Congress will have 12 cabinet slots, including the Chief Minister post, while 9 berths will go to the allies. The Muslim League will have five ministers. After days of internal negotiations, the Congress leadership has persuaded senior leader Ramesh Chennithala to accept the Home and Vigilance portfolios. Chennithala, who had been overlooked for the Chief Minister's post, had reportedly expressed dissatisfaction over the arrangement. Sources said the issue surrounding the Home portfolio was resolved on Friday, though Chennithala is believed to have sought greater representation for his loyalists in both the Cabinet and the party organisation. With the Home portfolio likely to go to Chennithala, Satheesan may choose the Finance portfolio, given his emphasis on fiscal issues during his tenure as Leader of the Opposition. IUML national general secretary P K Kunhalikutty is almost certain to return to the ministry as the party's senior-most leader. Other names under consideration include K M Shaji, N Shamsudheen, V E Abdul Gafoor and P K Basheer. Party sources indicate that Kunhalikutty is likely to handle the Industries and IT portfolios. Other departments expected to go to IUML ministers include Education, Public Works, Local Self-Government and Urban Affairs. Shibu Baby John of the Revolutionary Socialist Party (RSP) had earlier said talks with the Congress leadership were positive and that the party was not insisting on any particular portfolio. CMP's lone MLA C P John is also expected to be accommodated in the ministry. Meanwhile, former CPM leader G Sudhakaran said on Saturday that he would neither seek nor decline a ministerial berth if offered. The swearing-in of newly elected MLAs is tentatively scheduled for May 21, while the election to the Speaker's post is expected to be held on May 22.
